## CV-Builder Project Status Report

### Current Phase Status

**Currently Complete:** Phases 1–15 ✅  
**Next Phase:** Post-Phase-15 Polish & Integration Work  
**Most Recent Commits:** 4 unpublished commits ahead of origin/main (last commit: `dd2698e`, feat(code-quality): DTO response types, concurrency guards, and sendMessage refactor)

### Detailed Phase Summary

| Phase | Title | Status | Completion Date | Key Artifacts |
|-------|-------|--------|-----------------|----------------|
| 1-4 | LLM Integration & Core Orchestration | ✅ Complete | Prior | `cv_orchestrator.py`, `llm_client.py` |
| 5-8 | Content Customization & Rewrite Analysis | ✅ Complete | Prior | Rewrite feature, persuasion checks, achievement ordering |
| 9 | Synonym Map & Bullet Reordering | ✅ Complete | Prior | `synonym_map.json`, achievement reorder UI |
| 10 | Persuasion Checks + Generation Progress | ✅ Complete | 2026-03-11 | Rule-based verb/filler phrase checks, progress polling |
| 11 | Frontend Modularization | ✅ Complete | 2026-03-11 | `web/utils.js`, `web/api-client.js`, `web/state-manager.js`, `web/ui-core.js`, `web/styles.css` |
| 12 | Natural-Language Layout Instructions | ✅ Complete | 2026-03-11 | Layout review phase, iframe preview, instruction persistence |
| 13 | Master CV Management Tab | ✅ Complete | 2026-03-11 | UI tab for Master CV editing & upload |
| 14 | Cover Letter Generation Tab | ✅ Complete | 2026-03-11 | Cover letter generation + customization |
| 15 | Screening Questions Response Tab | ✅ Complete | 2026-03-11 | Screening questions UI + response management |

### Test Suite Status

- **Total Tests**: 308 (Python) + 104 (JavaScript) = **412 tests**
- **Python Tests**: All passing (308/308) ✅
- **JavaScript Tests**: All passing (104/104) ✅
- **Coverage**: Vitest + jsdom for frontend; pytest for backend
- **Last Full Run**: Passing (see Phase 12 completion log)

### Web Architecture (Post-Phase-11 Refactoring)

**Frontend Structure:**
- `web/index.html` (15.5 KB) — Modular markup with tab routing, modal management
- `web/app.js` (316 KB) — Main application controller, tab handlers, API orchestration
- `web/ui-core.js` (33.6 KB) — Tab routing, modal management, page initialization
- `web/api-client.js` (7.2 KB) — Centralized HTTP layer with error handling
- `web/state-manager.js` (10.1 KB) — Session lifecycle + localStorage persistence
- `web/utils.js` (5.1 KB) — Pure utility functions
- `web/styles.css` (44 KB) — Organized stylesheet (components + layout)
- `web/layout-instruction.js` (9.9 KB) — Natural-language layout instructions
- `web/media/` — Static assets

### Critical Open Gaps & Blockers

**HIGH Severity (blocking features):**
- **GAP-14**: Workflow Progress Indicator — Phase visualization not yet implemented in UI
- **GAP-15**: Accessibility Baseline — WCAG criteria (focus management, aria labels, keyboard nav) not specified
- **GAP-18**: Workflow Stage Re-run — Users cannot re-run completed phases while preserving downstream state
- **GAP-06**: Rewrite Review Card UI — Card layout spec not finalized; some UX details pending

**MEDIUM Severity (notable omissions):**
- **GAP-01**: Master Data NL Update — Natural-language update path unspecified; session harvest path only partially done
- **GAP-02**: Iterative Refinement — Phase back-transition logic unspecified
- **GAP-03**: Finalise Flow — Git commit + Drive sync triggers not implemented
- **GAP-04**: Post-Generation ATS Validation Report — 16-point validation spec not in REQUIREMENTS.md
- **GAP-05**: CV Length Estimation & Warning — Page count checks missing
- **GAP-08**: Spell/Grammar Check — API endpoint + UI panel not yet planned in implementation roadmap
- **GAP-16**: UX Evaluation Stories — No REQUIREMENTS.md specs for US-U2–U8 criteria

### Configuration & Environment

- **Conda Env**: `cvgen` (Python 3.12.12)
- **Web Server**: Flask on 127.0.0.1:5000 (default)
- **LLM Provider**: Requires `--llm-provider` flag or `config.yaml` setting (supports: `copilot-oauth`, `copilot`, `github`, `openai`, `anthropic`, `gemini`, `groq`, `local`)
- **Data Paths**:
  - Master CV: `~/CV/Master_CV_Data.json`
  - Publications: `~/CV/publications.bib`
  - Output: `~/CV/files/` (sessions, metadata, generated outputs)

### Key Commands

**Start Web App:**
```bash
python scripts/web_app.py --llm-provider github
```

**Run Tests:**
```bash
# Python (all categories):
python run_tests.py

# JavaScript (frontend):
npm run test:js              # All tests
npm run test:js:watch       # Watch mode
npm run test:js:cover       # With coverage
```

### Git Status

- **Branch**: `main`
- **Ahead of origin**: 4 commits
  - `dd2698e` feat(code-quality): DTO response types, concurrency guards, sendMessage refactor
  - `54f74f3` fix+refactor: code review fixes, token cap removal, test migration

- **Uncommitted Changes**:

### Project Files (Root-Level Documentation)

- `/ARCHITECTURE.md` — System design & component relationships
- `/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` — 15-phase implementation log with design decisions
- `/PROJECT_SPECIFICATION.md` — User stories & acceptance criteria

### Key Directories

- `/scripts/` — Python backend (orchestrator, LLM client, web app)
- `/web/` — JavaScript frontend modules + HTML
- `/tests/` — Python unit + integration tests; JavaScript tests in `tests/js/`
- `/tasks/` — Working documentation (gaps.md, user stories)
- `/.github/` — GitHub workflows, obo_helper sessions
- `/templates/` — HTML/DOCX CV templates

### Next Steps / Recommended Work

1. **Implement GAP-14** (Workflow Progress Indicator) — High visibility, foundational UX
2. **Define GAP-15** (Accessibility) — Add to REQUIREMENTS.md; audit existing UI
3. **Implement GAP-18** (Workflow Re-run) — Phase re-entry with downstream state preservation
4. **Spec GAP-04** (Post-Generation Validation) — Add 16-point validation checks
5. **Polish Phase 15** — Ensure all tabs (Master CV, Cover Letter, Screening) are fully integrated
6. **Push commits** — 4 commits ready to merge to origin/main

```text
     1→# Copilot Instructions for cv-builder
     2→
     3→## 🛑 CRITICAL: NEVER LOSE USER DATA
     4→
     5→1. **NEVER delete, drop, or destroy user data** by error, omission, or negligence
     6→2. **ALWAYS get explicit confirmation** for ANY action that destroys data
     7→3. **Create backups before irreversible operations** (session files, `Master_CV_Data.json`, generated output under `~/CV/`)
     8→4. **When in doubt, ASK**
     9→
    10→Actions requiring explicit confirmation:
    11→- Overwriting or deleting `Master_CV_Data.json`, `publications.bib`, or session files
    12→- Any `rm -rf` or destructive file operations under `~/CV/`
    13→- Any `--force` flag on git operations
    14→
    15→---
    16→
    17→## Big picture (read first)
    18→- This is a **single-user local CV generation app**: web UI + CLI, no DB, file-backed state.
    19→- Primary flow: job description → LLM analysis/recommendations → user review/edit → CV generation.
    20→- Source of truth is local files, especially `~/CV/Master_CV_Data.json` and `~/CV/publications.bib` (see `config.yaml` and `scripts/utils/config.py`).
    21→- Main runtime boundaries:
    22→  - Web/API: `scripts/web_app.py`
    23→  - Orchestration/generation: `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py`
    24→  - Conversation state/session logic: `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py`
    25→  - Provider abstraction: `scripts/utils/llm_client.py`
    26→
    27→## Critical workflows
    28→- Environment: use conda env `cvgen`.
    29→- Start web app: `python scripts/web_app.py --llm-provider github`.
    30→- If `--llm-provider` is omitted, `llm.default_provider` must be configured via env/config.
    31→- zsh tip: if shell autocorrect changes `github` to `.github`, escape or quote the provider value, e.g. `python scripts/web_app.py --llm-provider \github` (or `--llm-provider 'github'`).
    32→- Start CLI flow: `python scripts/llm_cv_generator.py`.
    33→- Run Python tests via orchestrator script (preferred):
    34→  - `python run_tests.py`
    35→  - `python run_tests.py --categories unit component integration`
    36→  - `python run_tests.py --list`
    37→- Run JavaScript tests (frontend utilities in `web/`):
    38→  - `npm run test:js`              — run all 104 JS tests
    39→  - `npm run test:js:watch`        — watch mode
    40→  - `npm run test:js:cover`        — with coverage
    41→  - Test files: `tests/js/utils.test.js`, `tests/js/api-client.test.js`, `tests/js/state-manager.test.js`
    42→  - Config: `vitest.config.mjs`; stack: Vitest 3 + jsdom
    43→
    44→## Project-specific patterns and gotchas
    45→- Config precedence is intentional and must be preserved: env vars > `.env` > `config.yaml` > defaults (`scripts/utils/config.py`).
    46→- Session persistence is file-based (`~/CV/files/sessions` by default); avoid introducing hidden in-memory-only state for core workflow.
    47→- `skills` in master data can be either a list or category dict; existing code handles both—keep that compatibility.
    48→- Recommendation semantics are strict: each recommendation includes `recommendation`, `confidence`, and `reasoning` with project-specific enums (see prompt logic in `scripts/utils/llm_client.py` and `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py`).
    49→- `scripts/llm_cv_generator.py` CLI currently restricts `--llm-provider` choices to `github|openai|anthropic|local`, while backend factory supports more providers (`copilot-oauth`, `copilot`, `gemini`, `groq`). Keep changes consistent when touching provider UX.
    50→- **Single-session architecture**: the Flask app has one global `ConversationManager` / `CVOrchestrator`. A `threading.Lock` enforces single-session-at-a-time. Avoid patterns that make multi-session keying harder.
    51→- **Rewrite audit key**: field is `final_text` in the spec but `final` in code (renamed in commit `576b75f`). Do not revert.
    52→
    53→## Configuration
    54→
    55→All configuration is in `config.yaml` (project root). **Precedence: env vars > `.env` > `config.yaml` > code defaults.**
    56→
    57→**There is no built-in default LLM provider** — the app fails on startup if `llm.default_provider` is unset.
    58→
    59→```yaml
    60→data:
    61→  master_cv:    string   # Path to Master_CV_Data.json. Default: ~/CV/Master_CV_Data.json
    62→  publications: string   # Path to publications.bib.  Default: ~/CV/publications.bib
    63→  output_dir:   string   # Root output dir.           Default: ~/CV/files
    64→
    65→llm:
    66→  default_provider: string   # REQUIRED. One of: copilot-oauth | copilot | github |
    67→                             #   openai | anthropic | gemini | groq | local
    68→  default_model:    string   # Model name; null = provider default.
    69→  temperature:      float    # Default: 0.7
    70→  max_tokens:       int|null # null = provider default.
    71→
    72→generation:
    73→  max_skills:        int   # Default: 20
    74→  max_achievements:  int   # Default: 5
    75→  max_publications:  int   # Default: 10
    76→  formats:
    77→    ats_docx:   bool  # Default: true
    78→    human_pdf:  bool  # Default: true
    79→    human_docx: bool  # Default: true
    80→
    81→session:
    82→  auto_save:    bool    # Default: true
    83→  session_dir:  string  # Default: ~/CV/files/sessions
    84→  history_file: string  # Default: ~/CV/files/input_history
    85→
    86→google_drive:
    87→  enabled:          bool    # Default: false (use OS-level Google Drive Desktop sync instead)
    88→  credentials_path: string
    89→  token_path:       string
    90→
    91→web:
    92→  host:  string  # Default: 127.0.0.1
    93→  port:  int     # Default: 5000
    94→  debug: bool    # Default: false
    95→
    96→logging:
    97→  level:   string      # DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR. Default: INFO
    98→  file:    string|null # null = console only.
    99→  log_dir: string      # Default: ~/CV/logs
   100→```
   101→
   102→`--llm-provider` and `--llm-model` flags on `web_app.py` / `llm_cv_generator.py` override `config.yaml` values.
   103→
   104→## Integration points
   105→- LLM providers are selected through `get_llm_provider(...)` in `scripts/utils/llm_client.py`.
   106→- Copilot OAuth flow is exposed by `/api/copilot-auth/*` endpoints in `scripts/web_app.py` and uses `utils/copilot_auth.py` token caching.
   107→- URL ingestion in web flow includes protected-site handling (LinkedIn/Indeed/Glassdoor) with manual-copy fallback in `scripts/web_app.py`.
   108→- Document generation uses WeasyPrint (primary) and Chrome headless (fallback) for PDF output; do not assume Quarto is installed.
   109→

   111→- Respect VS Code **user-level** agent instructions/prompts/skills symlinked into:
   112→  - `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/`
   113→  - Managed from `~/src/vscode-config` via `setup-symlinks.sh`.
   114→- Treat this file as project-specific guidance; user-level instructions still apply unless they conflict with explicit repo requirements.

   116→## Available Slash Commands
   117→
   118→Slash commands are defined in `~/src/vscode-config/.github/prompts/`. The most relevant:
   119→
   120→| Command | Purpose |
   121→|---------|---------|
   122→| `/preflight` | Mandatory pre-flight (run before every response) |
   123→| `/plan` | Create structured implementation plan → walk through via `/obo` |
   124→| `/obo` | One-by-one sequential item processor with priority scoring and session persistence |

   127→| `/commitMessage` | Generate thematic git commit messages |

   129→
   130→### /obo in cv-builder

   132→- Helper script: `~/src/vscode-config/.github/skills/one-by-one/obo_helper.py`

   143→- Never pass item JSON directly as a shell argument — write to a temp file and pass via `--input-file`

   406→
   407→### Design Decisions (Phase 9)
   408→
   409→**D9.1 — Synonym map is a flat JSON file, not hard-coded.**
   410→`scripts/data/synonym_map.json` is auto-loaded at orchestrator startup and can be extended without touching Python. A missing file degrades gracefully to `{}`.
   411→
   412→**D9.2 — Synonym matching is bidirectional via a single expansion index.**
   413→Both directions (alias→canonical and canonical→alias) are added to `_expansion_index`. ATS keywords are expanded before scoring, so a skill named `ML` matches keyword `Machine Learning` and vice versa. Terminology never changes — only matching.
   414→
   415→**D9.3 — Alias deduplication merges entries but preserves original names as aliases.**
   416→When `ML` and `Machine Learning` both appear in master data, the merge produces a single entry named `Machine Learning` (canonical) with `aliases: ['ML']`. The original name is not lost and appears in the CV template's tooltip.
   417→
   418→**D9.4 — Auto bullet sorting defaults to keyword-token overlap.**
   419→No LLM call is needed. A closure counts how many tokens (after synonym expansion) in each bullet text overlap with `ats_keywords`. This is fast, deterministic, and auditable. User-explicit ordering always takes precedence.
   420→
   421→**D9.5 — User bullet ordering is stored outside `customizations`.**
   422→`state['achievement_orders']` is persisted alongside spell-check and rewrite audit data. It is injected into `customizations` just before `generate_cv()` so the orchestrator receives it cleanly without polluting the LLM-facing recommendation structure.
   423→

   428→Full suite: 272 passed, 1 warning in 4.33s

Added `check_persuasion(experiences)` method: analyses selected bullets for weak verbs, missing metrics, vague language, and length. Added per-step `generation_progress` tracking in `generate_cv()` — records `step`, `status`, and `elapsed_ms` for each of the three output formats. |
   443→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | Added `persuasion_warnings: []` and `generation_progress: []` to session state. Added `run_persuasion_checks(rewrites, job_analysis, master_data)` method — applies the 8 LLMClient checks to each proposed rewrite text, collects failures as warning dicts. `re_run_phase()` now stores `persuasion_warnings` alongside `pending_rewrites`. |
   444→| `scripts/web_app.py` | `/api/status`: Added `generation_progress` and `persuasion_warnings` fields to response. `/api/rewrites` (`POST`): Calls `run_persuasion_checks()` and returns `persuasion_warnings` in payload. Added `GET /api/persuasion-check`: re-derives selected experiences from session state and returns persuasion findings + summary. |
   445→| `web/index.html` | Added CSS: `#loading-progress-bar` (fixed top stripe, 3 px, animated fill), `step-pulse` `@keyframes`, `.step.active.loading-step`. Enhanced `setLoading(loading, label)`: creates/removes bar, toggles pulse on active step. `renderRewritePanel(rewrites, warnings)`: builds collapsible warning box (grouped by type, expandable rows, Acknowledged button) prepended to panel. `fetchAndReviewRewrites()`: extracts `persuasion_warnings` from `/api/rewrites` response, announces count in chat. Generation progress polling: after `generate_cv` action, polls `/api/status` every 500 ms for up to 2 min, streams `✓`/`⏳` step indicators into conversation message. `persuasionWarningsAcknowledged` flag tracks UI review state. |
   446→| `tests/test_llm_client.py` | Added `TestPersuasionChecks` (36 tests): covers all 8 check methods with pass/fail/empty-string edge cases. |

   448→
   449→### Design Decisions (Phase 10)
   450→
   451→**D10.1 — Persuasion checks are rule-based; no LLM call.**
   452→All 8 checks in `LLMClient` use regex and vocabulary lookups, not LLM inference. This keeps check latency <1 ms per rewrite and makes results deterministic and testable. The checks are intentionally lenient — partial-match thresholds and `info`-severity results prevent false positives from flagging legitimate bullets.
   453→
   454→**D10.2 — Warnings are advisory, not blockers.**
   455→`persuasionWarningsAcknowledged` tracks whether the user reviewed the warnings panel, but the "Submit decisions" button is not hard-gated on acknowledgement. Warnings surface friction points for the user's awareness; workflow progression is never blocked by them.
   456→
   457→**D10.3 — `generation_progress` is stored in session state and polled.**
   458→`state['generation_progress']` records each generation step post-completion. The frontend polls `/api/status` every 500 ms after firing `generate_cv` and streams live step indicators into the conversation message. Storing in session state makes progress available on page reload without re-running generation.
   459→
   460→**D10.4 — Two separate persuasion check entry points for different scopes.**
   461→`LLMClient.check_*` static methods check proposed rewrite text before the user approves it (rewrite-review phase). `CVOrchestrator.check_persuasion()` analyses finalized selected bullets after generation (post-generation analysis tab). Both use rule-based heuristics from overlapping vocabulary definitions, but are invoked at different points in the workflow.
   462→
   463→**D10.5 — `/api/persuasion-check` re-derives content at request time.**
   464→Instead of caching selected content in session state (large dict), the endpoint re-runs `_select_content_hybrid()` lazily. This keeps session JSON lean and is idempotent — the same inputs always produce the same selected content.
   465→

   485→| `web/index.html` | Added `tab-layout` tab to viewer tab bar. Activated `step-layout` (removed `upcoming` class and added `onclick`). |
   486→| `web/app.js` | Removed `layout` from `UPCOMING` set. Added `layout` to `STEP_LABELS`. Added `layout` to `done` dict (true when phase is `'refinement'`). Added `'layout_review': 'layout'` to `phaseToStep`. Added `layout: 'layout'` to `stepToTab` in `handleStepClick`. Added `case 'layout': initiateLayoutInstructions()` to `loadTabContent`. Post-generation flow now calls `switchTab('layout')` and restores layout tab on session reload. |
   487→| `web/styles.css` | Added `/* ===== LAYOUT INSTRUCTIONS ===== */` section (~30 lines): two-column panel, preview iframe container, input pane, textarea, spinner, confirmation message, history list, entry styles. |
   488→| `tests/test_conversation_manager.py` | Added `TestCompleteLayoutReview` (6 tests): phase advance, empty/non-empty instructions stored, return values, `None` treated as empty, `_save_session` called. |
   489→| `tests/test_layout_instructions.py` | Updated `test_layout_phase_in_step_mapping` to expect `'layout_review'` (was `'layout'`). |
   490→| `tests/ui/test_ui_workflow.py` | Added `'tab-layout'` to `test_all_tabs_rendered` assertion list. |
   491→
   492→### Design Decisions (Phase 12)
   493→
   494→**D12.1 — `layout_review` is a first-class phase, not part of `refinement`.**
   495→Inserting `layout_review` between `generation` and `refinement` keeps the phase state machine consistent with the 8-step workflow bar. `complete_layout_review()` already advanced to `'refinement'`; the only change was making `generate_cv` land in `'layout_review'` instead of jumping straight to `'refinement'`.
   496→
   497→**D12.2 — Post-generation navigation goes to layout tab, not CV preview.**
   498→After `sendAction('generate_cv')` completes, `switchTab('layout')` is called instead of `switchTab('cv')`. The CV HTML is still pre-loaded into `tabData.cv` so switching to the Generated CV tab remains instant. This puts the user at the right next step without an extra click.
   499→
   500→**D12.3 — Layout tab initialized lazily via `initiateLayoutInstructions()`.**
   501→The layout panel HTML is injected on first tab activation, not at page load. This avoids loading iframe content before any CV exists. Subsequent activations check for the `.layout-instruction-panel` sentinel element and skip re-initialization.
   502→
   503→**D12.4 — ATS DOCX layout scope: LLM-decided (Q5 resolved).**
   504→Per the Q5 decision recorded in Phase 11: layout instructions for the ATS DOCX are not hard-restricted to text-only changes. The LLM system prompt includes ATS constraints (no multi-column, no custom fonts, no text boxes) and is responsible for deciding which visual instructions are safe to apply. This keeps the interface uniform while preventing dangerous ATS modifications.
   505→
   506→**D12.5 — `undoInstruction` is a stub; undo deferred.**
   507→Full undo requires re-applying all prior instructions from the base HTML up to step N-1. This is a significant LLM re-call chain. The stub records the intent and shows a system message; full implementation is deferred to a future polish pass.
   508→

   303→## GAP-15: Accessibility Baseline — Not Specified Anywhere
   304→
   305→**Severity:** HIGH  
   306→**Affected stories:** US-U7  
   307→**Status:** Not in REQUIREMENTS.md, PROJECT_SPECIFICATION.md, or any implementation file
   308→
   309→**Description:**  
   310→US-U7 defines a full accessibility baseline required for the web UI:
   311→- All interactive elements must have a visible, styled focus indicator (no global `outline: none`)
   312→- Modal dialogs must trap focus while open and restore focus to the opener on close
   313→- Icon-only buttons must have `aria-label` or `title` attributes
   314→- Status information (accept/reject) must not be conveyed by colour alone — must also have text label or icon
   315→- Tab order must be logical and match visual reading order
   316→- Form validation errors must be associated with inputs via `aria-describedby`
   317→
   318→No accessibility requirements exist anywhere in the current specification or implementation. All web UI
   319→work currently in progress (Phase 5+ of rewrite-feature.md) could be built without these requirements
   320→and require retrofit.
   321→
   322→**Recommended resolution:**  
   323→- Add an `### Accessibility Requirements` subsection to REQUIREMENTS.md §7 or a standalone §8, listing
   324→  the US-U7 criteria as minimum implementation requirements for all UI components
   325→- Include as a checklist item in the Phase 5 (web UI) implementation plan in `tasks/rewrite-feature.md`
   326→- Run axe or Lighthouse accessibility audit before marking any Phase 5 task complete
   327→

   329→
   330→## GAP-16: UX Evaluation Stories — No Implementation Specs in REQUIREMENTS.md
   331→
   332→**Severity:** MEDIUM  
   333→**Affected stories:** US-U2, US-U3, US-U4, US-U5, US-U6, US-U8  
   334→**Status:** Evaluation criteria exist in user stories; no corresponding REQUIREMENTS.md spec sections; no implementation
   335→
   336→**Description:**  
   337→The UX expert stories define concrete acceptance criteria for every major UI surface, but none of their
   338→requirements are reflected in REQUIREMENTS.md or the Phase 5 implementation plan:
   339→
   340→| Story | Requirement with no spec |
   341→|-------|--------------------------|
   342→| US-U2 | Protected-site contextual guidance; fetch spinner within 300 ms; inline-editable extracted fields |
   343→| US-U3 | Analysis chunked into ≥4 distinct visual sections; keyword rank visualisation; mismatch above the fold |
   344→| US-U4 | Bulk accept/reject for tables >8 rows; inline row expansion without navigation; reorder controls without hover |
   345→| US-U5 | Inline red/green diff for rewrites (not side-by-side boxes); edit path without destroying diff view |
   346→| US-U6 | Step-labelled generation progress; inline PDF preview iframe; multiple version labels |
   347→| US-U8 | Layout functional at 1280×800; skeleton placeholders for async content; ≤2 s shell load |
   348→
   349→**Recommended resolution:**  
   350→- For each story, add a corresponding `### UI: {Component}` subsection to REQUIREMENTS.md §7 lifting
   351→  the acceptance criteria into the spec (they are already written — this is transcription work)
   352→- Prioritise US-U3 (analysis readability), US-U5 (rewrite diff), and US-U4 (review tables) as these
   353→  are prerequisites for Phase 5 implementation work already in progress
   354→
   355→---
   356→
   357→## GAP-17: Persuasion Artefacts — Not Defined Anywhere
   358→
   359→**Severity:** MEDIUM  
   360→**Affected stories:** US-P3, US-P4, US-P6  
   361→**Status:** Required by persuasion expert acceptance criteria; not in REQUIREMENTS.md, config.yaml, or any implementation file
   362→
   363→**Description:**  
   364→Three artefacts are referenced by persuasion expert stories but do not exist anywhere in the project:
   365→
   366→1. **Strong action verb list** (US-P4) — US-P4 acceptance criterion: *"Every proposed bullet begins with a verb from an approved strong-action-verb list."* No such list is defined. Required fields: verb stem, category (leadership, technical, delivery, analysis), minimum seniority level.
   367→
   368→2. **Filler phrase / weak language list** (US-P3, US-P4) — US-P3 requires flagging summaries with more than one generic filler phrase; US-P4 requires flagging bullets with passive or weak constructions. Phrases to include: "results-driven", "responsible for", "helped to", "assisted with", "was involved in", "passionate about", "team player", "detail-oriented". No list is defined.
   369→
   370→3. **CAR (Challenge-Action-Result) structure detection** (US-P3) — US-P3 requires the system to identify bullets that already contain challenge language and propose CAR restructuring. No detection logic or prompt specification exists.
   371→
   372→**Recommended resolution:**  
   373→- Add a `strong_action_verbs` list to `config.yaml` (or a standalone `config/persuasion_rules.yaml`) with ~60 approved verbs grouped by category
   374→- Add a `weak_phrases` list to the same config file covering the filler/passive constructions above
   375→- Add a `### Persuasion Quality Checks` subsection to REQUIREMENTS.md §1 or §6 specifying:
   376→  - Action verb validation: run on every proposed bullet before presenting for review
   377→  - Filler phrase detection: run on summary and cover letter; flag if count > 1
   378→  - CAR detection: LLM prompt that identifies challenge clause presence in a bullet and proposes restructuring if found
   379→- All three checks are warn-level (surfaced for user decision), not hard-fail
   380→
   381→---
   382→
   383→## GAP-18: Workflow Stage Re-run — Not Specified, Not Implemented
   384→
   385→**Severity:** HIGH
   386→**Affected stories:** US-A12, US-U1, US-A6
   387→**Status:** Not in REQUIREMENTS.md; no implementation in `conversation_manager.py` or `web/index.html`
   388→
   389→**Description:**
   390→US-A12 requires that users can re-run any completed earlier workflow stage (e.g., re-trigger job analysis
   391→after proceeding to customisations) without silently discarding downstream approved decisions. This is
   392→distinct from US-A6 (post-generation iterative refinement) and US-U1 (general back-navigation safety):
   393→US-A12 specifically addresses *intentional re-processing* of a step — for example, re-running the LLM
   394→analysis when the user suspects it was incomplete or used wrong clarification answers.
   395→
   396→Current gaps:
   397→
   398→- `conversation_manager.py` only advances phases forward; no `re_run_phase()` or `back_to_phase()` logic
   399→  exists that preserves downstream state.
   400→- The progress indicator in `web/index.html` does not expose a re-run affordance on completed stages.
   401→- No spec for how a re-run LLM call should receive downstream approved decisions as context.
   402→- No spec for diff-highlighting which items changed as a result of the re-run vs. items unaffected.
   403→- Overlaps with GAP-02 (back-transition logic) but is broader: GAP-02 covers post-generation re-entry;
   404→  GAP-18 covers re-running any step at any point in the workflow, including mid-workflow.
   405→
   406→**Recommended resolution:**
   407→
   408→- Add `### Workflow Stage Re-run` subsection to REQUIREMENTS.md §7 specifying:
   409→  - Which stages support re-run (all completed stages)
   410→  - Confirmation dialogue content: affected downstream stages listed, preservation guarantee stated
   411→  - LLM re-run context: must include original job text, current clarification answers, and a compact
   412→    summary of all downstream approved decisions
   413→  - Diff highlighting: after re-run, items that changed vs. prior run are flagged; unchanged items
   414→    retain approved state without requiring re-confirmation
   415→- Implement `conversation_manager.re_run_phase(phase_name)` that:
   416→  - Saves current downstream state as a checkpoint before re-running
   417→  - Resets only the target phase and marks downstream phases as "needs review" (not "approved")
   418→  - Provides rollback if user cancels after seeing re-run results
   419→- Add re-run controls to the progress indicator in `web/index.html` (visible on completed stage chips)
   420→- Amend the `/api/analyze` endpoint (and equivalent phase endpoints) to accept optional
   421→  `downstream_context` payload when called as a re-run
   422→- Session audit log must record each re-run event: phase name, timestamp, changed item count
   423→
```

### Web Architecture (Post-Phase-11 Refactoring)

- `web/index.html` (15.5 KB) — Modular markup with tab routing, modal management

- `web/api-client.js` (7.2 KB) — Centralized HTTP layer with error handling
- `web/state-manager.js` (10.1 KB) — Session lifecycle + localStorage persistence
- `web/utils.js` (5.1 KB) — Pure utility functions

- `/PROJECT_SPECIFICATION.md` — User stories & acceptance criteria

- `/CLAUDE.md` — Points to copilot-instructions.md (main reference)
- `/copilot-instructions.md` — Critical project guidance (commands, patterns, gotchas)

### Key Directories

- `/scripts/` — Python backend (orchestrator, LLM client, web app)
- `/web/` — JavaScript frontend modules + HTML
- `/tests/` — Python unit + integration tests; JavaScript tests in `tests/js/`
- `/tasks/` — Working documentation (gaps.md, user stories)
- `/.github/` — GitHub workflows, obo_helper sessions
- `/templates/` — HTML/DOCX CV templates

5. **Polish Phase 15** — Ensure all tabs (Master CV, Cover Letter, Screening) are fully integrated
6. **Push commits** — 4 commits ready to merge to origin/main

     6→This document identifies gaps between what the user stories require and what is currently specified or implemented.
     7→Each gap has a severity, affected user stories, and a recommended resolution.
     8→
     9→**Severity scale:**  
    10→`CRITICAL` — blocks a user story's acceptance criteria  
    11→`HIGH` — significantly degrades the experience; should be resolved before that feature ships

    13→`LOW` — nice-to-have clarification or polish

    17→## GAP-01: Master Data NL Update — Partially Resolved
    18→

    25→natural-language input ("I just finished a project at Acme using Kubernetes. Add it to exp_005.") or by
    26→pasting an existing document (old CV, LinkedIn export). The system should show a proposed JSON diff and
    27→require explicit confirmation before writing.
    28→
    29→US-A11 (Session Master Data Harvest) now specifies the session-end structured write-back path, which
    30→partially addresses this gap. The freeform NL update and document ingestion path (US-A10) remains
    31→unspecified: no REQUIREMENTS.md section, no NL→JSON diff UI spec, no document-ingestion parse step.
    32→
    33→**Recommended resolution:**  
    34→Add a `### X. Master Data Management` section to REQUIREMENTS.md covering:
    35→- NL update → proposed JSON diff → confirm → write + git commit + Drive sync
    36→- Document ingestion → extract structured data → review step → confirm
    37→- Out-of-scope triggers (malformed input, ambiguous field mapping)
    38→- Cross-reference US-A11 harvest flow for the session-end structured path (already specified)

    43→
    44→**Severity:** HIGH

    51→experience) — not a full session restart. Previously approved decisions must be preserved as defaults on

    56→- What "preserved as defaults" means in session state when re-entering a prior phase
    57→- Whether partial regeneration (re-render only the affected section) is supported
    58→
    59→`conversation_manager.py` currently only advances phases forward; no back-transition logic exists.
    60→
    61→**Recommended resolution:**

    63→- Add `back_to_phase(target_phase)` to `conversation_manager.py` that resets state to the target phase while preserving prior decisions

   290→- Stage indicator must update without a full page reload
   291→
   292→`web/index.html` has no workflow stage indicator component. REQUIREMENTS.md has no UI specification for
   293→this element. This is the primary orientation mechanism for a multi-step workflow.
   294→
   295→**Recommended resolution:**  
   296→- Add a `### Workflow Progress Indicator` subsection to REQUIREMENTS.md §7 (Workflow & User Experience)
   297→  specifying: stage list, active/completed/pending visual states, back-nav confirmation trigger rules
   298→- Implement as a persistent header or sidebar component in `web/index.html` driven by session phase state
   299→- Cross-reference phase state in `conversation_manager.py` as the source of truth for current stage
   300→
   301→---
   302→
   303→## GAP-15: Accessibility Baseline — Not Specified Anywhere
   304→
   305→**Severity:** HIGH  
   306→**Affected stories:** US-U7  
   307→**Status:** Not in REQUIREMENTS.md, PROJECT_SPECIFICATION.md, or any implementation file
   308→
   309→**Description:**  
   310→US-U7 defines a full accessibility baseline required for the web UI:
   311→- All interactive elements must have a visible, styled focus indicator (no global `outline: none`)
   312→- Modal dialogs must trap focus while open and restore focus to the opener on close
   313→- Icon-only buttons must have `aria-label` or `title` attributes
   314→- Status information (accept/reject) must not be conveyed by colour alone — must also have text label or icon
   315→- Tab order must be logical and match visual reading order
   316→- Form validation errors must be associated with inputs via `aria-describedby`
   317→
   318→No accessibility requirements exist anywhere in the current specification or implementation. All web UI
   319→work currently in progress (Phase 5+ of rewrite-feature.md) could be built without these requirements
   320→and require retrofit.
   321→
   322→**Recommended resolution:**  
   323→- Add an `### Accessibility Requirements` subsection to REQUIREMENTS.md §7 or a standalone §8, listing
   324→  the US-U7 criteria as minimum implementation requirements for all UI components
   325→- Include as a checklist item in the Phase 5 (web UI) implementation plan in `tasks/rewrite-feature.md`
   326→- Run axe or Lighthouse accessibility audit before marking any Phase 5 task complete
   327→
   328→---
   329→
   330→## GAP-16: UX Evaluation Stories — No Implementation Specs in REQUIREMENTS.md
   331→
   332→**Severity:** MEDIUM  
   333→**Affected stories:** US-U2, US-U3, US-U4, US-U5, US-U6, US-U8  
   334→**Status:** Evaluation criteria exist in user stories; no corresponding REQUIREMENTS.md spec sections; no implementation
   335→
   336→**Description:**  
   337→The UX expert stories define concrete acceptance criteria for every major UI surface, but none of their
   338→requirements are reflected in REQUIREMENTS.md or the Phase 5 implementation plan:
   339→
   340→| Story | Requirement with no spec |
   341→|-------|--------------------------|
   342→| US-U2 | Protected-site contextual guidance; fetch spinner within 300 ms; inline-editable extracted fields |
   343→| US-U3 | Analysis chunked into ≥4 distinct visual sections; keyword rank visualisation; mismatch above the fold |
   344→| US-U4 | Bulk accept/reject for tables >8 rows; inline row expansion without navigation; reorder controls without hover |
   345→| US-U5 | Inline red/green diff for rewrites (not side-by-side boxes); edit path without destroying diff view |
   346→| US-U6 | Step-labelled generation progress; inline PDF preview iframe; multiple version labels |
   347→| US-U8 | Layout functional at 1280×800; skeleton placeholders for async content; ≤2 s shell load |
   348→
   349→**Recommended resolution:**  
   350→- For each story, add a corresponding `### UI: {Component}` subsection to REQUIREMENTS.md §7 lifting
   351→  the acceptance criteria into the spec (they are already written — this is transcription work)
   352→- Prioritise US-U3 (analysis readability), US-U5 (rewrite diff), and US-U4 (review tables) as these
   353→  are prerequisites for Phase 5 implementation work already in progress
   354→
   355→---
   356→
   357→## GAP-17: Persuasion Artefacts — Not Defined Anywhere
   358→
   359→**Severity:** MEDIUM  
   360→**Affected stories:** US-P3, US-P4, US-P6  
   361→**Status:** Required by persuasion expert acceptance criteria; not in REQUIREMENTS.md, config.yaml, or any implementation file
   362→
   363→**Description:**  
   364→Three artefacts are referenced by persuasion expert stories but do not exist anywhere in the project:
   365→
   366→1. **Strong action verb list** (US-P4) — US-P4 acceptance criterion: *"Every proposed bullet begins with a verb from an approved strong-action-verb list."* No such list is defined. Required fields: verb stem, category (leadership, technical, delivery, analysis), minimum seniority level.
   367→
   368→2. **Filler phrase / weak language list** (US-P3, US-P4) — US-P3 requires flagging summaries with more than one generic filler phrase; US-P4 requires flagging bullets with passive or weak constructions. Phrases to include: "results-driven", "responsible for", "helped to", "assisted with", "was involved in", "passionate about", "team player", "detail-oriented". No list is defined.
   369→
   370→3. **CAR (Challenge-Action-Result) structure detection** (US-P3) — US-P3 requires the system to identify bullets that already contain challenge language and propose CAR restructuring. No detection logic or prompt specification exists.
   371→
   372→**Recommended resolution:**  
   373→- Add a `strong_action_verbs` list to `config.yaml` (or a standalone `config/persuasion_rules.yaml`) with ~60 approved verbs grouped by category
   374→- Add a `weak_phrases` list to the same config file covering the filler/passive constructions above
   375→- Add a `### Persuasion Quality Checks` subsection to REQUIREMENTS.md §1 or §6 specifying:
   376→  - Action verb validation: run on every proposed bullet before presenting for review
   377→  - Filler phrase detection: run on summary and cover letter; flag if count > 1
   378→  - CAR detection: LLM prompt that identifies challenge clause presence in a bullet and proposes restructuring if found
   379→- All three checks are warn-level (surfaced for user decision), not hard-fail
   380→
   381→---
   382→
   383→## GAP-18: Workflow Stage Re-run — Not Specified, Not Implemented
   384→
   385→**Severity:** HIGH
   386→**Affected stories:** US-A12, US-U1, US-A6
   387→**Status:** Not in REQUIREMENTS.md; no implementation in `conversation_manager.py` or `web/index.html`
   388→
   389→**Description:**
   390→US-A12 requires that users can re-run any completed earlier workflow stage (e.g., re-trigger job analysis
   391→after proceeding to customisations) without silently discarding downstream approved decisions. This is
   392→distinct from US-A6 (post-generation iterative refinement) and US-U1 (general back-navigation safety):
   393→US-A12 specifically addresses *intentional re-processing* of a step — for example, re-running the LLM
   394→analysis when the user suspects it was incomplete or used wrong clarification answers.
   395→
   396→Current gaps:
   397→
   398→- `conversation_manager.py` only advances phases forward; no `re_run_phase()` or `back_to_phase()` logic
   399→  exists that preserves downstream state.
   400→- The progress indicator in `web/index.html` does not expose a re-run affordance on completed stages.
   401→- No spec for how a re-run LLM call should receive downstream approved decisions as context.
   402→- No spec for diff-highlighting which items changed as a result of the re-run vs. items unaffected.
   403→- Overlaps with GAP-02 (back-transition logic) but is broader: GAP-02 covers post-generation re-entry;
   404→  GAP-18 covers re-running any step at any point in the workflow, including mid-workflow.
   405→
   406→**Recommended resolution:**
   407→
   408→- Add `### Workflow Stage Re-run` subsection to REQUIREMENTS.md §7 specifying:
   409→  - Which stages support re-run (all completed stages)
   410→  - Confirmation dialogue content: affected downstream stages listed, preservation guarantee stated
   411→  - LLM re-run context: must include original job text, current clarification answers, and a compact
   412→    summary of all downstream approved decisions
   413→  - Diff highlighting: after re-run, items that changed vs. prior run are flagged; unchanged items
   414→    retain approved state without requiring re-confirmation
   415→- Implement `conversation_manager.re_run_phase(phase_name)` that:
   416→  - Saves current downstream state as a checkpoint before re-running
   417→  - Resets only the target phase and marks downstream phases as "needs review" (not "approved")
   418→  - Provides rollback if user cancels after seeing re-run results
   419→- Add re-run controls to the progress indicator in `web/index.html` (visible on completed stage chips)
   420→- Amend the `/api/analyze` endpoint (and equivalent phase endpoints) to accept optional
   421→  `downstream_context` payload when called as a re-run
   422→- Session audit log must record each re-run event: phase name, timestamp, changed item count
   423→

Try wkhtmltopdf if available\n        try:\n            cmd = ['wkhtmltopdf', '--page-size', 'Letter', str(html_file), str(pdf_output)]\n            subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, timeout=30)\n            return True\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return False\n    \n    def _create_conversion_diagnostic(self, html_file: Path, pdf_output: Path, cv_data: Dict) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Create diagnostic information when PDF conversion fails.\"\"\" \n        diagnostic_content = f\"\"\"PDF CONVERSION DIAGNOSTIC REPORT\nGenerated: {datetime.now().isoformat()}\n{'='*50}\n\nHTML FILE STATUS:\n- File exists: {html_file.exists()}\n- File size: {html_file.stat().st_size if html_file.exists() else 'N/A'} bytes\n- File path: {html_file}\n\nCV DATA SUMMARY:\n- Personal info: {'✓' if cv_data.get('personal_info', {}).get('name') else '❌'}\n- Experiences: {len(cv_data.get('experiences', []))} items\n- Skills: {len(cv_data.get('skills_by_category', []))} categories\n- Education: {len(cv_data.get('education', []))} items\n\nCONVERSION ATTEMPTS:\n1. WeasyPrint - Failed (ensure 'pip install weasyprint' completed)\n2. Chrome Headless - Failed (ensure Google Chrome installed)\n3.     else:
        wp_warnings: List[str] = []

        class _WPCapture(_logging.Handler):
            def emit(self, record: _logging.LogRecord) -> None:
                wp_warnings.append(record.getMessage())

        wp_logger = _logging.getLogger('weasyprint')
        _handler  = _WPCapture()
        _handler.setLevel(_logging.WARNING)
        wp_logger.addHandler(_handler)
        try:
            import weasyprint as _wp
            html_str  = html_path.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')
            rendered  = _wp.HTML(string=html_str,
                                 base_url=str(html_path.parent)).render()
            page_count = len(rendered.pages)
            _chk('html_renders_ok', 'HTML renders without error', 'pdf', 'pass',
                 f'Rendered {page_count} page(s) successfully')
            clip_warns = [w for w in wp_warnings
                          if 'clip' in w.lower() or 'overflow' in w.lower()]
            if not clip_warns:
                _chk('pdf_no_clipping', 'No WeasyPrint clipping warnings', 'pdf', 'pass',
                     'No clipping or overflow warnings')
            else:
                _chk('pdf_no_clipping', 'No WeasyPrint clipping warnings', 'pdf', 'warn',
                     f'{len(clip_warns)} clipping warning(s): {clip_warns[0][:100]}')
        except Exception as exc:
            _chk('html_renders_ok', 'HTML renders without error', 'pdf', 'fail',
                 f'WeasyPrint error: {str(exc)[:200]}')
            _chk('pdf_no_clipping', 'No WeasyPrint clipping warnings', 'pdf', 'fail',
                 'HTML render failed')
        finally:
            wp_logger.removeHandler(_handler)

    # ── PDF size check 14 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if pdf_path is None:
        _chk('pdf_us_letter', 'PDF page size is US Letter', 'pdf', 'fail', 'PDF file not found')
    else:
        try:
            import pypdf as _pypdf
            reader = _pypdf.PdfReader(str(pdf_path))
            if reader.pages:
                w = float(reader.pages[0].mediabox.width)
                h = float(reader.pages[0].mediabox.height)
                # Normalise to portrait
                w, h = min(w, h), max(w, h)
                if abs(w - 612) < 6 and abs(h - 792) < 6:
                    _chk('pdf_us_letter', 'PDF page size is US Letter', 'pdf', 'pass',
                         f'{w:.0f}×{h:.0f} pts — Letter')
                elif abs(w - 595) < 6 and abs(h - 842) < 6:
                    _chk('pdf_us_letter', 'PDF page size is US Letter', 'pdf', 'warn',

            else:
                _chk('pdf_us_letter', 'PDF page size is US Letter', 'pdf', 'fail', 'PDF has no pages')
        except Exception as exc:

# One-By-One Sequential Item Processor

Extract items from the current context and present them sequentially using intelligent prioritization. This system helps manage complex multi-item tasks by focusing on one item at a time with clear navigation.

## Process Overview

1. **Check for Existing Sessions**: Use `obo_list_sessions` for fast startup — do NOT read session files manually:

   **Step 1a — List sessions (fast path)**:
   - Call `obo_list_sessions(base_dir=<project_root>, status_filter="incomplete")`
   - Returns sessions where `status == "active"` AND `pending > 0` — these are resumable

   **Step 1b — Offer resume if incomplete sessions found**:
   - If resumable sessions exist, use `ask_questions` to present options:
     1. **Resume**: Continue from where the previous session left off
     2. **Merge**: Add new items to the previous session and resume
     3. **Defer**: Leave existing session for later and start a new session
     4. **Replace**: Mark existing session as completed and start fresh
     5. **Stop**: Stop /obo and let user handle manually
   - If no resumable sessions, proceed directly to item extraction

> **MCP unavailable?** Fall back to:
> `python3 ~/src/vscode-config/.github/skills/one-by-one/obo_helper.py <command> ...`
> Remove this note once `mcp.json` is updated to the GitHub URL (Phase C).

2. **Extract Items**: Analyze the current context to identify discrete items to process:
   - Todo list items
   - File contents or sections
   - Requirements or specifications
   - Code blocks or functions
   - Code review findings
   - Any enumerable tasks or components

3. **Calculate Priority Scores**: Evaluate each item using multiple factors:
   - **Urgency** (1-5): How time-sensitive is this item?
   - **Importance** (1-5): How critical is this to overall goals?
   - **Effort** (1-5): How much work is required? (lower effort = higher priority)
   - **Dependencies** (1-5): How many other items depend on this?
   - **Formula**: `priority_score = urgency + importance + (6 - effort) + dependencies`
   - Defaults when not specified: urgency=3, importance=3, effort=3, dependencies=1

4. **Persist Item List**: Create the session with `obo_create`:
   - Call `obo_create(base_dir=<project_root>, title=..., description=..., items=[...])`
   - Creates the session file and updates `index.json` atomically — no separate file writes needed
   - Items are passed as a list of dicts; priority scores are calculated automatically

5. **Analyze Dependencies and Order Items**: Identify dependencies between items and cascading impacts. Attempt to create a logical order based on dependencies (prerequisites first). If circular dependencies or conflicts prevent a single logical order, use `ask_questions` to alert the user and allow them to choose a different ordering approach (e.g., by priority score, alphabetical, or manual selection).

6. **Present Executive Summary**: Before sequential presentation, provide a high-level overview:
   - **Total Items**: Count of items to process
   - **Category Breakdown**: Group items by type/severity/area
   - **Priority Distribution**: How many high/medium/low priority items
   - **Estimated Effort**: Total effort required (sum of effort scores)
   - **Critical Items**: Count of urgent or blocking items
   - **Key Themes**: Common patterns or areas requiring attention
   - **Dependency Analysis**: Summary of dependencies and any ordering conflicts
   - **Recommended Approach**: Suggested order of operations

   **Example Summary**:
   ```
   ## Executive Summary

   **Total Items**: 12 findings
   **Categories**: 5 syntax errors, 4 anti-patterns, 3 optimizations
   **Priority Distribution**: 3 critical, 6 high, 3 medium
   **Estimated Effort**: 18 points (mix of quick fixes and moderate refactors)
   **Critical Items**: 2 data safety issues, 1 blocking syntax error
   **Key Themes**: Database connection management, parallel processing patterns
   **Dependency Analysis**: 3 items have dependencies; no conflicts detected
   **Recommendation**: Address 2 critical data safety issues first, then syntax errors, then optimizations
   ```

7. **Present Sequentially**: After summary, show the highest priority item with:
   - Clear focused context
   - Relevant background information
   - Specific actionable steps
   - Current progress indicator (e.g., "Item 3 of 12")

8. **Provide Navigation**: Use `ask_questions` tool to present clickable navigation buttons:
   - **Previous**: Return to previous item (if available)
   - **Approve Immediate**: Execute the described work for the current item immediately
   - **Approve Delayed**: Mark the current item as approved but delay execution until all items are reviewed
   - **Deny**: Deny current item and move to next
   - **Next**: Mark current item complete and move to next
   - **More Options**: Access additional navigation choices
   - **Elaborate**: Provide more detailed information about the current item
   - **List**: Show overview of all items with scores
   - **Summary**: Redisplay executive summary
   - **Reorder**: If ordering conflicts exist, allow user to choose different ordering approach
   - **Stop**: Stop /obo process and store current progress
   - **Help**: Display explanation of all navigation options
   - **Restore**: Reload original complete list from session file

---

## Session File Schema

Session files are plain JSON. Read and write them directly using the Read and Write tools.

**Path**: `.github/obo_sessions/session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json`

```json
{
  "session_file": "session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json",
  "created": "2026-03-10T14:30:00.000000",
  "title": "Human-readable session title",
  "description": "What this session is reviewing or working through",
  "status": "active",
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "phase-1",
      "title": "Short item title",
      "description": "Full description of the item and what needs to be done",
      "category": "General",
      "urgency": 5,
      "importance": 5,
      "effort": 2,
      "dependencies": 4,
      "priority_score": 18,
      "complexity": "S",
      "phase_deps": "none",
      "status": "pending",
      "resolution": ""
    }
  ]
}
```

**Item status values**: `pending` | `in_progress` | `completed` | `skipped`

**Session status values**: `active` | `completed`

**Optional item fields**: `complexity` (S/M/L), `phase_deps` (free text), `skip_reason` (populated on skip), `resolution` (populated on complete), `completed_at` (ISO timestamp on session close)

**Priority score formula**: `urgency + importance + (6 - effort) + dependencies`

---

## Session Operations (via MCP tools)

All session state changes go through MCP tools — do NOT use file tools (Read/Write/Bash)
for session JSON. The tools handle index.json updates automatically.

| Operation | MCP Tool Call |
|-----------|--------------|
| List sessions | `obo_list_sessions(base_dir, status_filter?)` |
| Create session | `obo_create(base_dir, title, description, items[])` |
| Session stats | `obo_session_status(session_file, base_dir?)` |
| Next item | `obo_next(session_file, base_dir?)` |
| List all items | `obo_list_items(session_file, base_dir?, status_filter?)` |
| Item detail | `obo_get_item(session_file, item_id, base_dir?)` |
| Mark complete | `obo_mark_complete(session_file, item_id, resolution, base_dir?)` |
| Skip item | `obo_mark_skip(session_file, item_id, base_dir?, reason?)` |
| Update field | `obo_update_field(session_file, item_id, field, value, base_dir?)` |

**`session_file`** accepts an absolute path or a bare filename (e.g. `session_20260314_120000.json`)
when `base_dir` is also provided.

**`obo_update_field`** auto-recalculates `priority_score` when `field` is `urgency`,
`importance`, `effort`, or `dependencies`.

### Mark item in-progress
Call `obo_update_field(session_file, item_id, "status", "in_progress", base_dir=...)`.

### Mark session complete
Call `obo_update_field(session_file, "session", "status", "completed", base_dir=...)`
— or update the session-level status directly; the tool handles `index.json`.

### Find next item to present
Call `obo_next(session_file, base_dir=...)` — returns `in_progress` items first
(highest priority_score), then highest-priority `pending`. Returns `{"message": "..."}` when done.

---

## Navigation Rules

- **Always use an interactive question tool for user input**: Any time user input or a decision is required, present structured choices — never wait for freeform text when options can be predefined
  - **VS Code Copilot Agent**: use `ask_questions`
  - **Claude Code**: use `AskUserQuestion` (exact equivalent)
- **Use the appropriate question tool for navigation** after presenting each item
  - **6-Option Limit**: both tools support maximum 6 options — use hierarchical [More Options] menus
  - **Primary Options** (main actions, shown first):
    1. Previous (return to previous item)
    2. Approve Immediate (execute work now)
    3. Approve Delayed (approve but delay execution)
    4. Deny (reject this item)
    5. Next (mark complete and continue)
    6. More Options (access secondary menu)
  - **Secondary Options** (meta-operations, shown after More Options):
    1. Elaborate (show more details)
    2. List (show all items with scores)
    3. Summary (redisplay executive summary)
    4. Reorder (change item ordering)
    5. Stop (save progress and exit)
    6. More Options (access tertiary menu if needed)
  - **Tertiary Options**:
    1. Help (explain all options)
    2. Save (flush all pending state changes to disk now)
    3. Restore (reload original list from session file)
    4. Back to Main Menu
  - **Option Bubbling**: Omit options not relevant to current context; allow others to move up
    - Omit "Previous" on first item → bubble up "Elaborate" or "Help"
    - Omit "Next" on last item if work incomplete → bubble up other options
    - Omit "Approve Immediate/Delayed" after already approved → bubble up
    - Omit "Reorder" if no ordering conflicts exist → bubble up
    - Omit "Restore" unless session was previously interrupted → bubble up
  - Use single-select mode; question header: "Navigate" or "Next Action"
- **When the user responds with freeform changes or additions** (rather than selecting a navigation option): Apply the requested changes to the relevant file(s), confirm what was changed, then re-present the **current item** with the navigation prompt — do NOT advance to the next item. Advancement only happens when the user explicitly selects Approve, Next, Deny, or Skip.
- **Write-on-change** — each MCP tool call writes to disk immediately (no batching needed):
  - Every `obo_mark_complete`, `obo_mark_skip`, and `obo_update_field` call persists to disk and updates `index.json`
  - No need to track unsaved state or show `(N unsaved)` — remove that indicator
  - "Save" in the tertiary menu can be omitted (or kept as a no-op confirmation)
- **Enable restoration**: When user selects Restore, Read the session file (last checkpoint) and re-display all items
- **Session cleanup**: When all items are finished, call `obo_update_field(session_file, "session", "status", "completed")` to mark the session complete. The tool updates `index.json` automatically.

**Item status values**: `pending` | `in_progress` | `completed` | `skipped`

**Session status values**: `active` | `completed`

**Optional item fields**: `complexity` (S/M/L), `phase_deps` (free text), `skip_reason` (populated on skip), `resolution` (populated on complete), `completed_at` (ISO timestamp on session close)

**Priority score formula**: `urgency + importance + (6 - effort) + dependencies`

---

## Session Operations (via MCP tools)

All session state changes go through MCP tools — do NOT use file tools (Read/Write/Bash)
for session JSON. The tools handle index.json updates automatically.

| Operation | MCP Tool Call |
|-----------|--------------|
| List sessions | `obo_list_sessions(base_dir, status_filter?)` |
| Create session | `obo_create(base_dir, title, description, items[])` |
| Session stats | `obo_session_status(session_file, base_dir?)` |
| Next item | `obo_next(session_file, base_dir?)` |
| List all items | `obo_list_items(session_file, base_dir?, status_filter?)` |
| Item detail | `obo_get_item(session_file, item_id, base_dir?)` |
| Mark complete | `obo_mark_complete(session_file, item_id, resolution, base_dir?)` |
| Skip item | `obo_mark_skip(session_file, item_id, base_dir?, reason?)` |
| Update field | `obo_update_field(session_file, item_id, field, value, base_dir?)` |

**`session_file`** accepts an absolute path or a bare filename (e.g. `session_20260314_120000.json`)
when `base_dir` is also provided.

**`obo_update_field`** auto-recalculates `priority_score` when `field` is `urgency`,
`importance`, `effort`, or `dependencies`.

### Mark item in-progress
Call `obo_update_field(session_file, item_id, "status", "in_progress", base_dir=...)`.

### Mark session complete
Call `obo_update_field(session_file, "session", "status", "completed", base_dir=...)`
— or update the session-level status directly; the tool handles `index.json`.

### Find next item to present
Call `obo_next(session_file, base_dir=...)` — returns `in_progress` items first
(highest priority_score), then highest-priority `pending`. Returns `{"message": "..."}` when done.

---

## Navigation Rules

- **Always use an interactive question tool for user input**: Any time user input or a decision is required, present structured choices — never wait for freeform text when options can be predefined
  - **VS Code Copilot Agent**: use `ask_questions`
  - **Claude Code**: use `AskUserQuestion` (exact equivalent)
- **Use the appropriate question tool for navigation** after presenting each item
  - **6-Option Limit**: both tools support maximum 6 options — use hierarchical [More Options] menus
  - **Primary Options** (main actions, shown first):
    1. Previous (return to previous item)
    2. Approve Immediate (execute work now)
    3. Approve Delayed (approve but delay execution)
    4. Deny (reject this item)
    5. Next (mark complete and continue)
    6. More Options (access secondary menu)
  - **Secondary Options** (meta-operations, shown after More Options):
    1. Elaborate (show more details)
    2. List (show all items with scores)
    3. Summary (redisplay executive summary)
    4. Reorder (change item ordering)
    5. Stop (save progress and exit)
    6. More Options (access tertiary menu if needed)
  - **Tertiary Options**:
    1. Help (explain all options)
    2. Save (flush all pending state changes to disk now)
    3. Restore (reload original list from session file)
    4. Back to Main Menu
  - **Option Bubbling**: Omit options not relevant to current context; allow others to move up
    - Omit "Previous" on first item → bubble up "Elaborate" or "Help"
    - Omit "Next" on last item if work incomplete → bubble up other options
    - Omit "Approve Immediate/Delayed" after already approved → bubble up
    - Omit "Reorder" if no ordering conflicts exist → bubble up
    - Omit "Restore" unless session was previously interrupted → bubble up
  - Use single-select mode; question header: "Navigate" or "Next Action"
- **When the user responds with freeform changes or additions** (rather than selecting a navigation option): Apply the requested changes to the relevant file(s), confirm what was changed, then re-present the **current item** with the navigation prompt — do NOT advance to the next item. Advancement only happens when the user explicitly selects Approve, Next, Deny, or Skip.
- **Write-on-change** — each MCP tool call writes to disk immediately (no batching needed):
  - Every `obo_mark_complete`, `obo_mark_skip`, and `obo_update_field` call persists to disk and updates `index.json`
  - No need to track unsaved state or show `(N unsaved)` — remove that indicator
  - "Save" in the tertiary menu can be omitted (or kept as a no-op confirmation)
- **Enable restoration**: When user selects Restore, Read the session file (last checkpoint) and re-display all items
- **Session cleanup**: When all items are finished, call `obo_update_field(session_file, "session", "status", "completed")` to mark the session complete. The tool updates `index.json` automatically.

# One-By-One Sequential Item Processor

Extract items from the current context and present them sequentially using intelligent prioritization. This system helps manage complex multi-item tasks by focusing on one item at a time with clear navigation.

## Process Overview

1. **Check for Existing Sessions**: Use `obo_list_sessions` for fast startup — do NOT read session files manually:

   **Step 1a — List sessions (fast path)**:
   - Call `obo_list_sessions(base_dir=<project_root>, status_filter="incomplete")`
   - Returns sessions where `status == "active"` AND `pending > 0` — these are resumable

   **Step 1b — Offer resume if incomplete sessions found**:
   - If resumable sessions exist, use `ask_questions` to present options:
     1. **Resume**: Continue from where the previous session left off
     2. **Merge**: Add new items to the previous session and resume
     3. **Defer**: Leave existing session for later and start a new session
     4. **Replace**: Mark existing session as completed and start fresh
     5. **Stop**: Stop /obo and let user handle manually
   - If no resumable sessions, proceed directly to item extraction

> **MCP unavailable?** Fall back to:
> `python3 ~/src/vscode-config/.github/skills/one-by-one/obo_helper.py <command> ...`
> Remove this note once `mcp.json` is updated to the GitHub URL (Phase C).

2. **Extract Items**: Analyze the current context to identify discrete items to process:
   - Todo list items
   - File contents or sections
   - Requirements or specifications
   - Code blocks or functions
   - Code review findings
   - Any enumerable tasks or components

3. **Calculate Priority Scores**: Evaluate each item using multiple factors:
   - **Urgency** (1-5): How time-sensitive is this item?
   - **Importance** (1-5): How critical is this to overall goals?
   - **Effort** (1-5): How much work is required? (lower effort = higher priority)
   - **Dependencies** (1-5): How many other items depend on this?
   - **Formula**: `priority_score = urgency + importance + (6 - effort) + dependencies`
   - Defaults when not specified: urgency=3, importance=3, effort=3, dependencies=1

4. **Persist Item List**: Create the session with `obo_create`:
   - Call `obo_create(base_dir=<project_root>, title=..., description=..., items=[...])`
   - Creates the session file and updates `index.json` atomically — no separate file writes needed
   - Items are passed as a list of dicts; priority scores are calculated automatically

5. **Analyze Dependencies and Order Items**: Identify dependencies between items and cascading impacts. Attempt to create a logical order based on dependencies (prerequisites first). If circular dependencies or conflicts prevent a single logical order, use `ask_questions` to alert the user and allow them to choose a different ordering approach (e.g., by priority score, alphabetical, or manual selection).

6. **Present Executive Summary**: Before sequential presentation, provide a high-level overview:
   - **Total Items**: Count of items to process
   - **Category Breakdown**: Group items by type/severity/area
   - **Priority Distribution**: How many high/medium/low priority items
   - **Estimated Effort**: Total effort required (sum of effort scores)
   - **Critical Items**: Count of urgent or blocking items
   - **Key Themes**: Common patterns or areas requiring attention
   - **Dependency Analysis**: Summary of dependencies and any ordering conflicts
   - **Recommended Approach**: Suggested order of operations

   **Example Summary**:
   ```
   ## Executive Summary

   **Total Items**: 12 findings
   **Categories**: 5 syntax errors, 4 anti-patterns, 3 optimizations
   **Priority Distribution**: 3 critical, 6 high, 3 medium
   **Estimated Effort**: 18 points (mix of quick fixes and moderate refactors)
   **Critical Items**: 2 data safety issues, 1 blocking syntax error
   **Key Themes**: Database connection management, parallel processing patterns
   **Dependency Analysis**: 3 items have dependencies; no conflicts detected
   **Recommendation**: Address 2 critical data safety issues first, then syntax errors, then optimizations
   ```

7. **Present Sequentially**: After summary, show the highest priority item with:
   - Clear focused context
   - Relevant background information
   - Specific actionable steps
   - Current progress indicator (e.g., "Item 3 of 12")

8. **Provide Navigation**: Use `ask_questions` tool to present clickable navigation buttons:
   - **Previous**: Return to previous item (if available)
   - **Approve Immediate**: Execute the described work for the current item immediately
   - **Approve Delayed**: Mark the current item as approved but delay execution until all items are reviewed
   - **Deny**: Deny current item and move to next
   - **Next**: Mark current item complete and move to next
   - **More Options**: Access additional navigation choices
   - **Elaborate**: Provide more detailed information about the current item
   - **List**: Show overview of all items with scores
   - **Summary**: Redisplay executive summary
   - **Reorder**: If ordering conflicts exist, allow user to choose different ordering approach
   - **Stop**: Stop /obo process and store current progress
   - **Help**: Display explanation of all navigation options
   - **Restore**: Reload original complete list from session file

---

## Session File Schema

Session files are plain JSON. Read and write them directly using the Read and Write tools.

**Path**: `.github/obo_sessions/session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json`

```json
{
  "session_file": "session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json",
  "created": "2026-03-10T14:30:00.000000",
  "title": "Human-readable session title",
  "description": "What this session is reviewing or working through",
  "status": "active",
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "phase-1",
      "title": "Short item title",
      "description": "Full description of the item and what needs to be done",
      "category": "General",
      "urgency": 5,
      "importance": 5,
      "effort": 2,
      "dependencies": 4,
      "priority_score": 18,
      "complexity": "S",
      "phase_deps": "none",
      "status": "pending",
      "resolution": ""
    }
  ]
}
```

**Item status values**: `pending` | `in_progress` | `completed` | `skipped`

**Session status values**: `active` | `completed`

**Optional item fields**: `complexity` (S/M/L), `phase_deps` (free text), `skip_reason` (populated on skip), `resolution` (populated on complete), `completed_at` (ISO timestamp on session close)

**Priority score formula**: `urgency + importance + (6 - effort) + dependencies`

How to register MCP server in Claude Code

```text
Perfect! I now have the complete official documentation. Let me provide you with a comprehensive answer based on Claude Code's MCP documentation.

## How to Register an MCP Server in Claude Code

Here are the answers to your four questions:

### 1. Command to Add an MCP Server

Use the `claude mcp add` command with appropriate flags for your server type:

```bash
# Local stdio server (Python script)
claude mcp add [options] <name> -- <command> [args...]

# Real example: Python MCP server
claude mcp add my-python-server -- python /path/to/server.py

# With environment variables
claude mcp add --env API_KEY=value my-python-server -- python /path/to/server.py --port 8000
```

The critical syntax rule: **All options (`--transport`, `--env`, `--scope`) must come BEFORE the server name. The `--` (double dash) separates options from the command.**

### 2. Where MCP Config Lives (Scopes)

MCP servers can be stored at three different scopes:

| Scope | Location | Visibility | Use Case |
|-------|----------|-----------|----------|
| **Local** (default) | `~/.claude.json` | You only, current project | Personal servers, experimental configs, sensitive credentials |
| **Project** | `.mcp.json` at project root | Team (checked into git) | Shared team tools, project-specific servers |
| **User** | `~/.claude.json` | You, all projects | Utilities needed across multiple projects |

```bash
# Default: local scope
claude mcp add my-server -- python server.py

# Explicitly set scope
claude mcp add --scope project my-server -- python server.py
claude mcp add --scope user my-server -- python server.py
```

### 3. Config Entry for Local Python Stdio Server

**Via CLI (simplest)**:
```bash
claude mcp add --scope project my-python-tool -- python /usr/local/bin/my_mcp_server.py --port 8000
```

**Or in `.mcp.json` (for team sharing)**:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "my-python-tool": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "python",
      "args": ["/usr/local/bin/my_mcp_server.py", "--port", "8000"],
      "env": {
        "API_KEY": "your-key-here"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**Key points for Python servers**:
- `type: "stdio"` for local process communication
- `command` is the Python interpreter (or full path if not in PATH)
- `args` includes the script path and any CLI arguments
- Environment variables go in the `env` object

### 4. Verify Registration and Test

Use these commands to verify the server is working:

```bash
# List all configured MCP servers
claude mcp list

# Get details for a specific server
claude mcp get my-python-tool

# Within Claude Code, check server status and authenticate if needed
/mcp

# Remove a server if needed
claude mcp remove my-python-tool
```

When you run `/mcp` inside Claude Code, you'll see:
- All configured servers and their status
- Available tools from each server
- Authentication options (if the server requires it)

### Pro Tips

1. **Test your server first**: Run the command manually to ensure it starts correctly:
   ```bash
   python /path/to/server.py
   ```

2. **Use absolute paths**: For both the script path and any config files referenced in args or env vars.

3. **Environment variable expansion**: If using `.mcp.json`, you can use `${VAR}` syntax for environment variables:
   ```json
   {
     "mcpServers": {
       "db": {
         "command": "python",
         "args": ["${HOME}/.mcp/servers/db.py"]
       }
    }
   }
   ```

4. **Timeout configuration**: If your Python server takes time to start, configure the startup timeout:
   ```bash
   MCP_TIMEOUT=10000 claude  # 10 second timeout
   ```

5. **Team setup**: Add to project scope (`.mcp.json`) and commit to git so all team members can use it automatically.

---

**Reference documentation**: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp.md
